title: Technical Professions
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Technical Professions
text:
Strengthening the Talent Pipeline Ecosystem
RAND researchers study strategies like regional partnerships and stackable credentials to help communities, employers, and training institutions develop and sustain a skilled, diverse workforce.
Requiem for a Visa
The H-1B visa, once the principal gateway for importing talent at scale, may be on the verge of becoming a legacy route—an expensive instrument reserved only for the rare, “distinguished” developer who fits the description envisioned more than 70 years ago.
Finding International Rockstar Researchers: Identifying Predictors of Success Following an International Move
After relocating internationally, some researchers thrive in their new environments, producing discoveries at rates higher than before their move. This report aims to identify predictors of successful outcomes after an international move.
Should the U.S. Lean into Short-Term, Nondegree Workforce Education and Training? Yes, but Carefully
Workforce development in the United States has increasingly emphasized flexible, fast, and focused education and training that enhances individual economic security. Some stakeholders are hoping Congress will lean further into this sort of education and training. But should we?
Assessing Needs for Civilian STEM Talent in the Department of the Air Force: Navigating the Complexities of Workforce Demand and Supply
The authors consider how the Department of the Air Force (DAF) can track the supply and demand of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) talent in the civilian workforce and identify potential gaps in technical competencies.
Building Impact: Perspectives and Recommendations on the Current State and Future of Architecture
In this report, the authors offer guidance to students, faculty, and practicing professionals in the architectural field on how to improve the relationship between education and practice, including the pathways to education and the workforce.
Evaluation of the Frontiers programme: Final evaluation report
RAND Europe was commissioned by the Royal Academy of Engineering to evaluate its Frontiers programme. This report assesses the programme's contributions, its added value and role in the wider ecosystem using a theory-based evaluation approach.
Military Spouse Employment in the Technology Sector: VetsinTech Roundtable Proceedings
In May 2023, VetsinTech hosted a policy roundtable to discuss the challenges that military spouses face when pursuing tech education, employment, and entrepreneurship and how the tech sector is uniquely positioned to mitigate these challenges.
Growing the Computer Science and Information Technology Workforces in Ohio
Using Ohio as a case study, the authors of this report offer suggestions to state leaders across the United States who are interested in growing and diversifying their computer science and information technology workforces.
